name: Grains Information - Whole Wheat
recipeInstructions:
Oven method: Preheat oven to 300°F. Combine whole wheat and water in a pan and bring to a boil. Boil for 5 minutes. Remove from heat, cover, and place in the oven. Turn off the oven and leave the wheat undisturbed overnight or for 8-10 hours. Use the cooked wheat for cereal, casseroles, bread, cookies, or other dishes. Store any leftover cooked whole wheat airtight in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
Split peas: Combine with other protein sources for better nutrition. Good in soups or as a vegetable. For quick soup, grind peas (whole or split) into flour by combining with water in a blender for 1 minute. Use a ratio of 1 part pea flour to 3-4 parts liquid. Simmer for 3 minutes and add seasonings. Add raw vegetables to the blender or soup. Ham flavoring is also an option.
Soybeans: Should be included as part of your storage plan due to their high protein content and nutritional value. They are rich in minerals, B vitamins, and unsaturated fats. Store dry soybeans in a cool, dry place and rotate every 3-5 years. To cook, soak soybeans overnight (3-4 cups water to 1 cup beans) in the refrigerator. If the beans are hard, boil for 2 minutes before soaking. For quicker cooking, boil for 2-5 minutes, remove from heat, cover, and let stand for 1-2 hours. If the soaking water is not bitter, use it to simmer the beans for 3 or more hours until tender. Use part of the cooked soybeans in chili recipes, bean salads, etc.
Proportions:
For whole wheat: Cook using a ratio of 1 part whole wheat to 2 parts water plus 1/2 - 1 teaspoon salt.
For split peas: Cook using a ratio of 1 part pea flour to 3-4 parts liquid.
For cooked whole or cracked wheat, use a ratio of 1 part cooked wheat to 4 parts water for coarse textured bread.
